headline: B40 Students 3 Times More Likely to Repeat Primary School Years
context: MOE internal data leaked in 2025 shows B40 children have triple the grade retention rate of M40 and T20 peers.

cards.big: 12% of B40 children repeat at least one primary school year versus 4% overall
cards.sub: Socioeconomic background remains strongest predictor of academic achievement

cards.big: B40 households spend RM42 monthly on education versus RM380 for T20 families
cards.sub: 9-fold spending gap covers tuition books and learning materials

cards.big: Free school meal program reaches 850,000 students but excludes 200,000 eligible children
cards.sub: Budget constraints leave coverage gaps in urban poor communities

cards.big: Brazil's Bolsa Familia reduced grade retention by 22% through conditional cash transfers
cards.sub: Financial support tied to school attendance improves educational persistence

cards.big: Grade retention punishes children for the poverty their parents cannot escape
Should Malaysia adopt conditional cash transfers tied to school attendance for B40 families
